title: Level 406.41
wikitext:

filter: grayscale(100%); } html { -webkit-filter: grayscale(100%); filter: grayscale(99%); } }} {{Background|file=Backdrop.png}}
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 406.41: The Unforgiving Halls…}} Well, we meet again, thirsty for knowledge I see, knowledge that you don’t need. You may know me, you may not. If you come here, you're going to forget anyway, that’s for sure. This place is a place you should never know about, a place where you should never visit. Some information is kept away for the best, you know? And this is a perfect example of that. This place you should not know about, it's a horrible place, another blind spot within the Backrooms. And if you see blood pooling where it is not invited, if you see a black-and-white room that should not be there, don’t enter unless you wish to lose who you are. Some places were never meant to be explored. This is that place. It takes who you are and leaves the rest mangled on the side of the road. There will be no light at the end of the tunnel. There is no pot of gold at the end of the rainbow. There is no victory if your only choice is to lose. I’m warning you now, if you go in there you will never see your friends again. If you go in there, you will never even have a chance of escaping. Even I, a person who hunts your kind, realize that this is somewhere I will never want to chance upon, because I know that they will never come out. Sometimes things need to be forgotten, and this level does just that and much more. Proceed with caution, not all corners are meant to be seen. Good luck, you're going to need it.
{{Fullscreen|show=>Data found, open file?|hide= X|content=
I see… you did not pay mind to my advice. Sad. Sometimes, I wish I turned back to my original ways. Though I can't put all of the blame on you. I was like that once: hungry for knowledge, hungry for power. I got what I wanted, yes, but did I lose everything that made me human? Yes. I sacrificed my life so I could know more, so I could have power. I don't think you want power, though, because I can tell you with 100% certainty that nothing good comes from entering here, only pain and suffering. You also may be asking me why I'm so keen to protect this document from you, even though it's not a cognitohazard. Well, it's because of a little something I call curiosity. Yes, that urge to dig deeper, and this level uses that to its advantage to lure in more prey. It's simple, yet effective. If it gives people just enough information to spark curiosity, but not enough to properly inform one, it will make someone stay up at night wondering what they're missing, what it's getting wrong. Because this document is inaccurate, to say the least. Some of the information is made up so that this page will stay up. Yes, a page will not be kept if it lacks information. Who will honestly read a page that's one or two sentences long? But that's just my two cents. It took a lot to get into this document to add my knowledge, but no matter what, I can't change your mind if you have a strong opinion. So I will leave you with this: read this page, and you can decide for yourself whether you think I'm right or the document is right. For all you know, I might just be trying to distract you from an exit to reality, to take your eyes away from a tiny hint in the passage that could harm me. Maybe I'm just trying to stir up confusion. That's all up to you. You already came this far, just finish what you started. Take one step closer, or take one step back. I won't care either way. Good luck, because you will need it.
{{Class 1|hexagon=Щ|class=Щ|color-h=54|color-s=70%|color-l=10%|safety=Deadly unsafe |security= Extreme Memory hazard|entity= Presence of one powerfully entity}} The Unforgiving Hallways is the 41 sub level, manifesting as an endless, multi-story mansion, drained of all color. == Description == If one comes into the level, they will notice that the level is completely drained of color, with black, white, and gray being the only colors that exist. Level 406.41 is dim, and it will take a while for one’s eyes to adjust to the darkness. As for the light sources, there are multiple unstable chandeliers that hang in random spots, along with candles that are lit and flicker along the halls. One can also find random light sources with no explanation, such as a room containing no visible light sources being bright. The structure of the level resembles a worn-down mansion, and it looks akin to a classic old black-and-white horror film, with cobwebs decorating every corner, and the smell of rotting wood drifting through the air. To accompany the looks, there is faint piano music that will constantly play through the level, though the exact music is unknown. People have tried to recreate it; all they know is that the music directly triggers fear in the wanderer. Other sounds in the level are often old creaking floorboards, random cracks and popping sounds, and even screams in the distance. Additionally, the level has a mind deterioration effect that causes one to forget continuously, though there are other things that cause one to lose memory quicker. There is always a constant forgetting effect throughout the whole level. Furthermore, nearly every wanderer that enters dies, and most communication devices bug out. Even if you did manage to get a device, you will forget who you were even talking to in no time, this is also why there are close to no pictures of the level. The level is assumed to be infinite because every door that a person encounters will lead to a new room, but due to little documentation and the death rate for a person entering, this is unconfirmed. Additionally, along with the forgetting effect, as soon as one enters, a chemical in the air known as A19R will make its way through one’s body and nervous system. As soon as the chemical reaches the brain (about 10 minutes after entering), the unfortunate wanderer will start to feel paranoia that worsens as time goes on Eventually, the paranoia will get so bad that hallucinations will start to manifest as the victim’s traumas and phobias, and in most cases, the victim dies after losing the ability to distinguish reality from illusion. Not only does the chemical effect your mind but it also affects your physical self because of how the chemical bonds with your body, your pain receptors get heightened so much as getting a paper cut will feel like you got a finger cut off, this also applies to your stamina and all muscle soreness will be heightened so running even 50% of a mile will feel like you ran a marathon, this is all to prevent wanderers from escaping. one last piece of advice: time and yourself is your worst enemy.
== The TV Event == Once a wanderer noclips into the level, they will be in a chair with a TV set upon a table. If the person starts to look around the table, they will see a VHS tape that they can insert into the bottom of the TV. It is highly advised to take the time to find and watch the tape because, if you do, you will know the rules and advice needed to maybe survive. If one is wise enough to take the time to listen, the information will prove very valuable because once the video starts to play, the following will be said:
”'''''Hello, if you wish to live, please consider the following rules.'''''”
After the TV is done displaying that, it will switch to a screen consisting of some rules that one can use to survive. One: Your only goal is to escape. You will be monitored closely to make sure you don't cheat; if you do, death will follow. Two: There are some doors that should never be approached, like one that leaks blood. If something seems off about a door, don't enter—trust your gut. Three: In some circumstances, if your vision regains the ability to see color again for about 30 seconds, you now only have 10 minutes left to escape. After the 30 seconds are over, however, your sight will go back to black and white. Four: Your main goal is to reach [[Level 106|Eden]] in black and white. While other exits do exist, they're not as good as this one. Five: Time is finite, though you will never know the exact limit. There are different times for each person who enters, but it will always be x > 15. Luckily for you, this tape did not take up any time. Once the rules are done being listed, the tape will eject, and wanderers will always remember those rules. No amount of mind deterioration will change that. Congratulations—you made the right choice.
== Notable Rooms == Notable rooms are all the rooms that make up the Unforgiving Halls. Each one has a different look to it, as well as rules and anomalies that are scrawled in a readable font on the back of each of the doors. These are similar to the general five rules that were on the tape but more room-specific. Although all of the rooms are different, they all share a faint smell of rot and decay, and signs of rot and disrepair are a common theme throughout all of the rooms. One can access these rooms randomly by entering a door. Different rooms are more common than others, and some rooms' doors have a higher chance to lead to other rooms. For example, if you are in the bedroom, the bathroom will be the most common room that you will see.
=== '''Hallway''' === The hallway is always the first thing anyone who noclips in sees. The hallway appears to be a long, narrow space that is worn down over time. The music here is very soft, nearly unbearable. The walls are covered in bland wallpaper, and one can feel something watching them even though there is nothing there. Along the walls are 14 doors, with 7 on each side. These doors lead to different sections of the level. The doors are very unremarkable and made of wood. The handles are made out of bronze, and the doors are 5’9, so taller people will have to crouch down. Most hallways are identical, with only where the doors lead being the identifying feature, though the hallway you noclipped into will always have the TV and desk. Rules and anomalies: * Oftentimes, pictures can appear pinned to the walls. Upon closer inspection, victims will realize that the pictures were drawn by them. Looking at them will cause intense paranoia, so don't look at them. * Remember to never enter doors that seem out of place; they will only open to your death. * If one finds a knife, do not touch it; your mind will become your biggest enemy. * Sometimes doors may lead to brick walls, and there is a 10% chance of that happening. If one is unlucky enough to get all brick walls, death will come soon. * Notes on the ground, called “death notes,” can be found on the floor. If one picks it up and reads it, whatever is written will happen to them word for word, and nothing good is ever written. * Most food that stays in here rots 2.5x faster.
=== '''Bedroom''' === The bedroom is by far the safest place to be in the Unforgiving Halls. The bedroom appears to be a mother's or a young girl's room, decorated with hearts and other art that girls will primarily like. This room is the least worn down, and most of the time only a few cracks and cobwebs can be found. If one enters this room, they will feel an overwhelming urge to stay. Survivors describe that this part of the level makes one get tired easily, making it even harder to leave. Even though the hazards here are very minimal, the amount of time that is wasted is immense, and on average it takes a good 5 minutes for one to escape this room, making it a big time waster. There are three doors on the right side of the room, and you will come through a door on the left side of the room. The door you came through will disappear. Rules and anomalies: * In extremely rare cases, hearts on the walls and bedsheets may resemble real human hearts. If this happens, run away from this room. * This room has rugs, and on rare occasions the rugs might trip wanderers, causing head damage. * Sometimes this room may have a window that plays static. If one sees this, look away, because staring at the window will cause the victim to lose memories, making it hazardous. * Generally safe unless anomalies manifest.
=== '''Rug Room''' === The Rug Room is a very odd room because it is not found in normal houses. The Rug Room is a 20’–20’–20’ room that has dusty rugs lining all walls and corners of the room. The room is extremely dangerous and should never be entered. Besides the Smilers, the rugs that line everything make the room very stuffy and hot, and paired with the dust, breathing is extremely hard. Additionally, the Rug Room is the only room that is known to contain entities. Because of the dim light, Smilers are common. All of the rugs contain a random liquid. It can be anything from the Backrooms or Frontrooms, so one time it can be almond water and another time it can be liquid pain. Entering this room without shoes is extremely discouraged. Doors are located in random locations around the walls, and 5 doors are always present. Rules and anomalies: * Wanderers report feeling watched in this room, though why is unknown because there is no one in here. * This room is not safe to stay in because of certain anomalies, and it contains a high amount of dust, making breathing hard. * The only room that contains entities: [[Entity 3|Smilers]] are the only ones.
=== '''Bathroom''' === The Bathroom is the most painful place one can enter. The room appears to be an old 1930s bathroom that has been worn down. Mold and rot can be found here, as well as a large amount of moisture, which supplies the mold to grow more. The bathroom, because of how heightened your pain sensors are, is extremely discouraged to enter because there are many painful hazards that one might find. The first and most blatant hazard is the bathtub. Not only does it splash around with no air or wind, but it is filled to the brim with liquid pain, and spills are common. Other than that, broken glass is common, as well as falling tiles from the ceiling that can hurt one. This room is advised to only be entered if one has protective items. Rules and anomalies: * The bathtub may appear in many locations in the bathroom. The tubs are filled to the brim with [[Object 12|liquid pain]]. * The sinks rarely dispense anything, and when they do dispense, only [[Object 44|Cashew Water]] comes out. * If one stares at a mirror, a person that is not you will look back. This will cause paranoia and death. * If mirrors leak blood, imminent death is unavoidable.
=== Book Room === The Book Room is the most hazardous and useful room. The room appears to be a relatively well-kept study. Rot and other signs of aging are minimal, and only cobwebs can be found. If one enters the room, a calming and curious mindset will take control of them, making them want to explore and open books. Please try not to do so. The Book Room has multiple good and bad anomalies. For example, there is a fire in the fireplace that burns cold. If one allows curiosity to take control and they touch the fire, they will be sent to a random deadzone level that will ensure the death of the poor victim. However, there are some good things, like green books that give you more time to try and escape. Rules and anomalies: * The fire in the pit is cold and intrigues curiosity. Don't touch it; doing so will send you to a random deadzone level. * There is a chair in the room. If one sits in it, they will get knocked out for about 10–15 minutes, making it a huge time waster. * The books in the room are not just for decoration. Instead, they have different effects if one opens and reads a word. 1.) The green book will give 5–10 extra minutes of time for you to escape. 2.) The red book will make the reader lose all function in one limb or origin. 3.) The black book will teleport one to the void. * Under very rare circumstances, books and paper too close to the fire may catch and cause a fire. As stated previously, the fire will send one to a deadzone level.
===Eye room=== Not much is known about this room because it is so rare to find, and everyone who enters it dies. However, it is safe to assume it is some entity's home. This entity has been thought to be the one who kills people who go over the time limit, and it is thought to be some type of black wall with an eye, but that is only a theory. Rules and anomalies: * The room is not only dangerous, but also very rare. * Home to a powerful entity. * Very little information has surfaced about this room, so everything about it is just theories.
== Entities == Smilers can rarely be found in the rug room. Besides that, there are no more confirmed entities, with an entity that kills all people who go past the time limit only being a theory. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the nature of the level, it is not possible to establish a colony. == Entrances and Exits == Entrances are only listed so wanderers know what to avoid and not enter. === '''Entrances''' === * When wandering too long in [[Level 0]], you might hear a scream, which will lead to a pool of blood. Slipping on it will cause one to noclip here. * There is a very small chance to noclip here if any part of [[Level 448|Level 448]] suddenly turns grey. * There is a chance that running into a broken light in [[Level 20.1|Level 20.1]] will lead one here. * Finding any of the rooms with blood in Level 406 will lead here. === '''Exits''' === * Reaching the black-and-white version of [[Level 106|Eden]] allows escape, though survival odds are near zero. * Going through the doo— Sorry but… can I stop you here? {{Collapse|num=10000000000000000000000000000000000000000|initial=[[File:Eye looking around.gif|thumb|375px|center]]|final=[[File:Eye looking around.gif|thumb|375px|center]]|body= Sorry, but this is where I stop you. I believe I allowed you to read enough of this bad article to supply your curiosity. And I wish I did not. I wish that I had just corrupted this article and written the real information. But it is too late, isn't it? You have read it and consumed the false knowledge. A shame; maybe one life just got lost right now because of the misinformation. But let me tell you, the level is more dangerous than it is stated in the passage, too horrible to put down into words. It is the definition of a deadzone, because no one will ever make it out. Trust me, I have been here. I am one of the few survivors. This is one of those places in the Backrooms that loves to kill for fun. This is one of the corners that should never be explored. It’s not just the memory loss; it is the pain. Even taking one step will feel like you fell 50 feet. You physically will not survive. I can see that you do not believe me. The danger and suffering are too extreme to be believable. I will let you believe what you want, as long as you do not come here. It’s no surprise that the Backrooms can do some horrible things, like Level ! or Level [REDACTED]. This takes the cake for suffering. The only mercy it gives? Is the guarantee of death. Because in a place like this, that mercy is the greatest gift of all. }} |padding=2rem 15%|number=1|color=#000000}}
